question: In the van der Waals equation $\left(P + \frac{a}{V^2}\right)(V - b) = RT$, what is the dimensional formula of the constant $a$?
In the van der Waals equation $\left(P + \frac{a}{V^2}\right)(V - b) = RT$, what is the dimensional formula of the constant $b$?
In the van der Waals equation $\left(P + \frac{a}{V^2}\right)(V - b) = RT$, what is the dimensional formula of the product $ab$?
In the equation $P = \frac{a - t^2}{bx}$, where $P$ is pressure, $x$ is distance, and $t$ is time, what is the dimensional formula of $a$?
In the equation $P = \frac{a - t^2}{bx}$, where $P$ is pressure, $x$ is distance, and $t$ is time, what is the dimensional formula of $b$?
In the equation $P = \frac{a - t^2}{bx}$, where $P$ is pressure, $x$ is distance, and $t$ is time, what is the dimensional formula of $a/b$?
If the velocity of a particle is given by $v = At + \frac{B}{t+C}$, what is the dimensional formula of $A$?
If the velocity of a particle is given by $v = At + \frac{B}{t+C}$, what is the dimensional formula of $B$?
If the velocity of a particle is given by $v = At + \frac{B}{t+C}$, what is the dimensional formula of $C$?
What is the dimensional formula of the permittivity of free space ($\epsilon_0$)?
What is the dimensional formula of the permeability of free space ($\mu_0$)?
The quantity $\frac{1}{\sqrt{\mu_0 \epsilon_0}}$ has the same dimensions as:
If $E$ is the electric field and $B$ is the magnetic field, what is the dimensional formula of $E/B$?
In an AC circuit, what is the dimensional formula of $CR$ (Capacitance $\times$ Resistance)?
In an electrical circuit, what is the dimensional formula of $L/R$ (Inductance / Resistance)?
What is the dimensional formula of $\sqrt{LC}$ where $L$ is inductance and $C$ is capacitance?
The quantity $\frac{1}{2} \epsilon_0 E^2$ (energy density of electric field) has the same dimensions as:
The magnetic energy density $\frac{B^2}{2\mu_0}$ has the dimensional formula:
What is the dimensional formula of thermal conductivity ($K$)?
What is the dimensional formula of Stefan-Boltzmann's constant ($\sigma$)?
What is the dimensional formula of the universal gas constant ($R$)?
What is the dimensional formula of the coefficient of thermal expansion ($\alpha$)?
What is the dimensional formula of the Solar Constant (energy received per unit area per unit time)?
What is the dimensional formula of the mechanical equivalent of heat ($J$)?
What is the dimensional formula of magnetic flux ($\Phi$)?
What is the dimensional formula of electric flux ($\Phi_E$)?
What is the dimensional formula of magnetic dipole moment ($M$)?
If Force ($F$), Velocity ($v$), and Time ($T$) are chosen as fundamental quantities, what is the dimensional formula of Mass?
If Energy ($E$), Velocity ($v$), and Time ($T$) are chosen as fundamental quantities, what is the dimensional formula of Surface Tension?
If Force ($F$), Velocity ($v$), and Time ($T$) are chosen as fundamental quantities, what is the dimensional formula of Work?
What is the ratio of the dimensions of Planck's constant ($h$) to the moment of inertia ($I$)?
What is the ratio of the dimensions of angular momentum to linear momentum?
The fine-structure constant is given by $\alpha = \frac{e^2}{4\pi\epsilon_0 \hbar c}$. What is its dimensional formula?

The number of particles crossing a unit area perpendicular to the x-axis per unit time is $N = -D \frac{n_2 - n_1}{x_2 - x_1}$, where $n$ is particles per unit volume. What is the dimensional formula of the diffusion constant $D$?
Reynolds number ($Re$) is a dimensionless quantity determining the nature of fluid flow. If $Re = \frac{\rho v d}{\eta}$, what is the dimension of the coefficient of viscosity $\eta$?

A physical quantity $X$ is given by $X = \frac{A^2 B^3}{C^{1/2}}$. If the percentage errors of measurement in $A, B$, and $C$ are 1%, 2%, and 4% respectively, what is the maximum percentage error in $X$?
If the error in the measurement of the momentum of a particle is $+2\%$, what will be the approximate error in its kinetic energy?
If the percentage error in the measurement of the radius of a sphere is $1.5\%$, what is the percentage error in the measurement of its volume?
The length and breadth of a rectangular sheet are $10.0 \pm 0.1$ cm and $5.0 \pm 0.1$ cm respectively. What is the percentage error in its area?

The time period of a simple pendulum is $T = 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{l}{g}}$. If the error in measuring length $l$ is $1\%$ and the error in measuring time period $T$ is $2\%$, what is the percentage error in the calculated value of $g$?
How many significant figures are there in the measured value $0.002500$ kg?
How many significant figures are present in the number $2.00 \times 10^5$?
Round off the value $15.75$ to three significant figures.
Round off the value $15.65$ to three significant figures.

The density of a material in the CGS system is $4 \mathrm{\ g/cm^3}$. In a system of units in which the unit of length is $10 \mathrm{\ cm}$ and unit of mass is $100 \mathrm{\ g}$, what is the numerical value of the density?
If the unit of force is $1 \mathrm{\ kN}$, the unit of length is $1 \mathrm{\ km}$, and the unit of time is $1 \mathrm{\ s}$, what will be the unit of mass in this new system?
A physical quantity is measured and its value is found to be $nu$ where $n$ is the numerical value and $u$ is the unit. Which of the following relations is true?
The displacement of a progressive wave is represented by $y = A \sin(\omega t - kx)$. What is the dimensional formula of $\omega/k$?
In the equation of state of an ideal gas $PV = nRT$, what is the dimensional formula of $PV$?
The velocity $v$ of water waves depends on the wavelength $\lambda$, density of water $\rho$, and acceleration due to gravity $g$. Deduce the relation using dimensions.
